title: Desktop state: register and normalize expert sessions for UI consumption
spec: Implement a single source of truth in the desktop renderer state for expert sessions created from start-expert-session flow. Ensure requested/starting/running expert sessions are captured from existing event channels, normalized with stable session IDs, and exposed through selectors that both sidebar and terminal views can consume without duplicating logic. Keep existing persistent-agent session behavior intact.
acceptance_tests:
AC1:
Given a new expert session is started and the backend reports it as requested or starting
When the desktop app receives the session event
Then the session appears in renderer state with a stable ID, role label, and lifecycle status.

AC2:
Given multiple session events for the same expert session arrive out of order
When the reducer/store applies updates
Then the final state keeps one canonical session record and the latest lifecycle status.

AC3:
Given only persistent agents (CPO/CTO) are present
When state initialization and updates run
Then existing persistent session visibility and selection behavior remain unchanged.

title: Desktop sidebar: show active expert sessions alongside persistent agents
spec: Update sidebar rendering to include expert sessions from shared session state. Add/restore an active sessions section that lists currently active expert sessions with readable role/session labels, and keep persistent agents visible and selectable. Ensure list updates reactively as session state changes and stale/ended sessions are removed according to lifecycle status.
acceptance_tests:
AC4:
Given an expert session exists in active lifecycle state
When the sidebar renders
Then the sidebar includes an active sessions section containing that expert session entry.

AC5:
Given a user starts an expert session from CPO context
When the session becomes visible in app state
Then the left sidebar no longer shows only CPO; it also shows the new expert session entry.

AC6:
Given an expert session transitions to a terminal/ended lifecycle state
When session state updates
Then the session is removed from active sidebar listings without removing persistent agents.

title: Desktop terminal routing: auto-switch to newly started expert session
spec: Implement auto-switch behavior so starting a new expert session moves terminal focus to that session once it is available, while preserving manual user selection and preventing unwanted focus steals. Add guard rails so auto-switch only triggers for newly created sessions in the current interaction flow, and does not trigger for historical/session replay updates.
acceptance_tests:
AC7:
Given a user starts a new expert session from the desktop app
When that session becomes available for interaction
Then terminal focus automatically switches to the new expert session.

AC8:
Given the user has manually switched to another session after expert start
When non-creation updates arrive for the expert session
Then the app does not repeatedly force focus back to the expert session.

AC9:
Given historical session hydration occurs during app load
When old expert sessions are loaded
Then auto-switch is not triggered for those historical sessions.
